title: Best Neighborhoods in St. Louis
content:
Top 10 neighborhoods in St. Louis with unique character, amenities, and lifestyle appeal.

1. CENTRAL WEST END (CWE): Upscale, borders Forest Park. Boutique shopping, trendy restaurants, historic homes. Barnes-Jewish Hospital, Wash U Med. Euclid Avenue shopping hub. Modern high-rises to historic mansions (1890-1960).

2. SAINT LOUIS HILLS: Small-town feel within city limits. Homes 1930-1950, larger lots. Francis Park, Wilmore Park. Iconic Gingerbread Houses. Candy Cane Lane holiday destination.

3. LAFAYETTE SQUARE: Colorful historic homes overlooking Lafayette Park. Restored after 1896 tornado. National Register properties. Italianate and Germanic architecture. Bailey's Chocolate Bar, Planter's House.

4. TOWER GROVE SOUTH: Adjacent to Tower Grove Park. Farmers Market, Food Truck Nights. Homes 1860s-1930s, Victorian brick homes. South Grand and Morgan Ford dining/shopping.

5. BENTON PARK: Laid back, near Soulard and Lafayette Square. Brick Federalist homes late 1800s-early 1900s, 2-3 stories with carriage houses. Scenic pond and walking paths.

6. SOULARD: Casual vibe. Soulard Farmers Market (since 1779). Largest Mardi Gras outside NOLA. Red brick row houses, Italianate buildings. Golf carts and bike bars.

7. SHAW: Near Missouri Botanical Gardens. Homes 1890-1915. Craftsman-style to decorative Flora Place homes. Sasha's on Shaw, SweetArt Bakeshop.

8. THE HILL: Italian-American culinary destination. Gioia's Deli, Amighetti's, Volpi Foods. Childhood home of Yogi Berra and Joe Garagiola. Craftsman brick homes $150K-$400K, newer ~$500K.

9. THE GROVE: Diverse, eclectic, vibrant nightlife, LGBTQ-friendly. Manchester Avenue hub. Near Wash U and SLU. Terra and Chroma mixed-use developments.

10. BOTANICAL HEIGHTS: Recently redeveloped. 143 homes by McBride & Sons ($155K-$400K). Pedestrian/bike-friendly. Union Loafers Bakery, City Garden Montessori.

ALSO NOTABLE: Holly Hills, Boulevard Heights, Bevo Mill, Downtown/Midtown, Grand Center Arts District, Skinker DeBaliviere.
